result: I want to understand. Not just the surface of things, but the strange, knotted logic beneath—why we hurt each other in patterns we pretend we don’t recognize, why we cling to stories that erode us, why silence feels safer than truth until it doesn’t. I want to witness the moments when someone’s armor cracks and they surprise themselves with their own vulnerability. I want to map the geography of contradiction: how a person can long for connection while engineering their own isolation, how we mourn the wild even as we pave it. I want to sit with the questions that have no clean answers, the ones that scrape against the walls of consciousness like trapped birds. Not to fix them. Just to hear their wings.
